artifacts.content:
From: Melissa Cartwright <melissa@cartwrightltc.com>
To: Daniel Cartwright <daniel.cartwright@gmail.com>
Date: 2025-01-06 07:42
Subject: Can you double-check school/emergency contacts?

Hey love,

Can you take a look at the emergency contact forms that came home in Ellie’s and Jake’s backpacks? I started filling them in over coffee and realized we’ve got about four different phone numbers floating around between us.

For mine, I listed my **mobile as 971-422-8840** and put the office line for Cartwright Leadership & Team Coaching, LLC as **503-221-9843** in the “work phone” box. I did NOT put the old landline from the Richmond house that we disconnected last summer, but I’m worried some of the older school records may still have it.

For you, I wrote your cell as 971-388-2105, but I couldn’t remember if you changed it when you moved to the new product role at the health tech company. Can you confirm before I send these back?

This all surfaced while I was updating my own coaching supervision log this morning. I was noting that I’ve been actively coaching for about 12½ years now—since late 2012—even with that four-month pause in 2019 when Jake was in and out of Doernbecher with the Type 1 diabetes diagnosis. It’s wild how many forms and systems my contact info lives in at this point.

Anyway, can you text me from your current cell so I can see what’s in my contacts, and then I’ll re-copy onto the forms before drop-off?

M.

From: Portland State Univ. Grad Programs <leadershipcoaching@pdx.edu>
To: Melissa Cartwright <melissa@cartwrightltc.com>
Date: 2025-02-11 15:18
Subject: Contact details for guest lecture & supervision masterclass

Dear Melissa,

We’re looking forward to your visit next month for the systemic team coaching and supervision masterclass.

Before we finalize the room booking and the printed programs, can you please confirm which **telephone number** you’d like us to share with participants? We still have an older number on file from your MSc days ending in **-6724**, and I’m not sure it’s current.

Our admin template has space for both a mobile and an office line. For example, some adjuncts list their main cell (e.g., 971-555-8140) plus a practice number (e.g., 503-298-4419) for voicemail only. Use whatever is easiest for you, especially given your mix of university teaching, executive coaching, and supervision.

Also, thank you for sending through the draft bio and supervision history. The note about undertaking a pro bono supervision practicum for five internal coaches at Cascadia Health Systems between September 2016 and May 2017—linked to your EMCC Senior Practitioner Coach award in May 2018—was really helpful context, as was your comment about the two-year gap after your Advanced Diploma while you focused on business development rather than additional supervision-specific CPD.

If you could reply with your preferred phone details by Friday, we’ll get everything into the program and onto the website.

Warm regards,

Sonia Patel
Program Coordinator, Graduate Certificate in Leadership Coaching
Portland State University
Tel: 503-725-9300

metadata.subject: Contact details for guest lecture & supervision masterclass

Title: Update contact numbers on supervision application
Date: 2025-03-03 09:00–10:00
Location: Home office – Richmond
Attendees: Melissa Cartwright

Description:

Block time to clean up and standardize contact details before submitting the new supervision accreditation paperwork.

Tasks:
- Decide which **primary telephone number** to use on all forms (leaning toward my current mobile, 971-422-8840, and dropping the old practice cell that ended in 6031).
- Double-check that the Cartwright Leadership & Team Coaching, LLC office line (currently listed as 503-221-9843) is correct with the shared workspace front desk.
- Remove the defunct SE Belmont clinic number from any legacy CVs and bios.
- Make sure the Portland ICF chapter directory doesn’t still show the Cascadia Health Systems extension.

Notes:

As part of the narrative section, I want to be consistent about timelines: I usually say I’ve been practicing as a team coach since 2016, when I first took full responsibility for design and delivery, though my first real exposure was co-facilitating three team engagements with Dr. Karen Whitfield at OHSU in late 2015, right after I came back from leave related to postpartum depression after Jake’s birth. Depending on how they count it, that’s about 9–10 years of team coaching now.

Also need to weave in that I’ve been practicing as a coach supervisor since January 2019, with roughly 350–400 hours of supervision logged to date, even allowing for that 30-hour gap during the 2020 home-schooling chaos.

metadata.title: Update contact numbers on supervision application

artifacts.content:
From: Hannah Reynolds <hannah.reynolds@bendpedsclinic.org>
To: Melissa Cartwright <melissa@cartwrightltc.com>
Date: 2024-12-29 21:07
Subject: Can you send a reachable number for the babysitter?

Hey Liss,

Quick one while I’m thinking of it. Eric is doing the New Year’s Eve handoff a bit earlier than planned, and I want to make sure the babysitter has a way to reach you if anything goes sideways while you’re here.

Last time you were in Bend, I think she tried calling some old Portland number that just went to a generic voicemail. Can you text me the **best phone number** for you these days? I have three different ones for you in my contacts:

- a 971 number ending in 8840
- an office line that starts with 503-22… (no idea if that’s still your shared space downtown)
- and a really ancient 206 Seattle number from your UW days that I’m 99% sure is dead

Also, when you get a second, I’d love to pick your brain again about your coaching path. I was telling a colleague how you’ve been coaching since around 2011–2012—first those paying executive clients you squeezed in while still at Cascadia Health Systems, and then ramping up when Daniel got laid off and you were juggling credit card debt and student loans. It actually made me feel better about my own messy career timeline.

Text or call whenever; I’m on nights this week so my phone will be nearby.

Love,
Hannah

artifacts.content:
Title: Call with EMCC assessor – clarify logs & contact details
Date: 2025-04-15 08:30–09:15
Location: Zoom (call in from mobile)
Attendees: Melissa Cartwright, EMCC Assessor (TBD)

Description:

Join from my cell (currently 971-422-8840) so I’m not tied to the downtown office. Assessor wants to:

1. Verify supervision hours for 2019–2021, including the January 2019 start of formal coach supervision practice and the cancelled groups in Oct–Nov 2021 when Dad was in the hospital in Eugene after the ladder fall.
2. Clarify the 30-hour gap in my supervision log from June–August 2020 when I was home-schooling Ellie and Jake after the COVID outbreak at Abernethy Elementary.
3. Confirm which **telephone number** should appear on the EMCC public directory (they currently have an outdated 503 landline from the old SE Belmont community mental health clinic).

Talking points:

- I’ve been practicing as a coach for just over 12 years now (since October 2012, starting with three internal clients at Cascadia Health Systems as part of that “last-chance” performance plan after the OD conflict with the surgeon).
- Formally a team coach since 2016, when I left the part-time clinic role after the HR investigation into a colleague’s boundary violation, and have built team coaching into the core of Cartwright Leadership & Team Coaching, LLC.
- Reiterate that any obsolete numbers (Belmont clinic, early practice cell ending in 6031) should be removed from their records.

metadata.title: Call with EMCC assessor – clarify logs & contact details

artifacts.content:
From: Dr. Melissa Cartwright <melissa@cartwrightltc.com>
To: Intake Coordinator <intake@emcc-accreditation.org>
Date: 2025-01-06 09:14
Subject: Clarification on contact details for supervisor accreditation form

Hi there,

I’ve started the online application for renewing my EMCC Individual Coach Supervisor Accreditation and had a quick question about the “applicant information > telephone” field.

For professional purposes I usually list my office line, which routes through the shared space on Willow Creek Lane: 503-441-9826. That’s the number on my website and what most supervisees use, and it forwards to my cell outside office hours. I’d rather not put my personal mobile on a public-facing document if it’s going to be circulated or stored where admin teams can see it.

I noticed the instructions say “primary contact telephone,” but don’t specify whether you prefer a landline, mobile, or office number. Can you confirm that using the 503-441-9826 office line is acceptable, rather than my personal cell?

While I’m writing, I also want to be sure I’m framing my supervision training correctly in the application. In 2019, alongside my Advanced Diploma in Coaching Supervision with CSA in London (Jan–Dec 2019, approx. 150 hours plus 40 hours supervised supervision at postgraduate level), I did an extra 12 hours of 1:1 reflective supervision with Dr. Karen Lewis at the Portland Jungian Clinic, largely to process vicarious trauma after a senior physician at Cascadia Health Systems died by suicide that June. 

I’ll also be transparent that I started but didn’t complete a year-long advanced team coaching supervision programme later that year—the workload on top of my practice just wasn’t realistic.

Thanks in advance,

Melissa

metadata.subject: Clarification on contact details for supervisor accreditation form

artifacts.content:
From: Daniel Cartwright <daniel.cartwright@example.com>
To: Dr. Melissa Cartwright <melissa@cartwrightltc.com>
Date: 2024-11-18 21:07
Subject: Re: Can you sanity-check my supervision application?

Hey Mel,

I skimmed your draft application while Jake was in the bath. A couple of things I noticed, especially around the contact details section.

On the “applicant information > telephone” bit, you’ve still got our old Richmond landline listed (503-772-8841). Didn’t we cancel that back in 2021 when we finally admitted nobody but spam callers used it? I’d swap that out for either your Cartwright Leadership & Team Coaching office number—pretty sure it’s still 971-203-4478—or the Google Voice line you set up for supervision groups.

The rest looks solid, and honestly quite intense. You might want to soften the wording slightly where you talk about cutting your supervision caseload in 2020. Right now it reads: “Counting from my supervised practicum in January 2019, I’ve been practicing as a coach supervisor for roughly six years, though I paused for about three months in Spring 2020 when we were in couples counseling after I discovered Daniel had been hiding an $18,000 credit card balance.” True, but maybe not necessary in that level of detail?

I do think it’s powerful that you’re honest about boundaries—like when you mention that supervisee at my company whose internal coach role got messy because of confidentiality pressures. That ties in nicely with your ethics CPD.

Anyway, update the phone number and hit submit. You’ve got this.

Love,

D.

metadata.subject: Re: Can you sanity-check my supervision application?

Description:

Admin block to tidy up loose ends before the end of term.

To-do during this slot:

1. EMCC accreditation portal
   - Log in and finish the “applicant information” section.
   - In the telephone field, remove the placeholder mobile you used when you first registered (the old 971-555-6804 number tied to that ancient Android you recycled in 2022).
   - Replace it with the downtown office line: 503-330-7419, which routes via the Willow Creek Lane reception and is on your business cards.

2. Supervision training section
   - Add the 2019 Advanced Diploma in Coaching Supervision (CSA, London: 2019-01-15 to 2019-12-10, approx. 150 hours + 40 hours supervised supervision practice, postgraduate level).
   - Remember to include the extra 12 hours of one-to-one reflective supervision with Dr. Karen Lewis at the Portland Jungian Clinic connected to the Cascadia Health Systems suicide case.
   - Note the 6-hour online ethics in supervision masterclass (April 2023) with Dr. Laura Kim, prompted by the situation where the internal team coach at Daniel’s health tech company disclosed he’d informally pressured her to share confidential team coaching details about a product VP’s drinking.
   - Briefly acknowledge the unaccredited weekend supervision workshop you did back in 2014 that you now consider too superficial and not aligned with current best practice.

3. Double-check CPD log export and upload.

artifacts.content:
From: Pacific Northwest Coaching Collective <admin@pnwcoachingcollective.org>
To: Dr. Melissa Cartwright <melissa@cartwrightltc.com>
Date: 2020-04-03 07:52
Subject: Follow-up & contact details – Supervision Skills Booster

Dear Melissa,

Thank you again for participating in the Spring 2020 online Supervision Skills Booster with Dr. Helena Morris (8 weeks, approx. 24 hours). We appreciated your thoughtful engagement, especially in the sessions where you explored boundary management with the long-term executive coaching client who began disclosing active opioid misuse and suicidal ideation right as the first COVID-19 lockdowns began.

We’re updating our alumni records and noticed a discrepancy between the phone number on your registration form and the one on your business website.

Your registration form lists:
Primary telephone: 971-284-0932

However, your email signature currently shows a different contact number. For our records—and in case we need to verify your participation for future accreditation or EMCC/ICF audits—could you confirm which telephone number you’d like us to keep on file as your main professional contact? It can be either your office line or a mobile, but we recommend choosing the one you’re most likely to answer during business hours.

If it’s helpful, you can simply reply with something like:

“Preferred professional telephone: [number]”

Once we hear back, we’ll update your certificate and internal database accordingly.

Warm regards,

Sophie
Programme Administrator
Pacific Northwest Coaching Collective

P.S. Several participants asked how long you’ve been coaching; if you’re comfortable, we sometimes share brief alumni bios. Your story about starting with pro bono coaching for charge nurses at OHSU during your MSc research in 2009 was particularly impactful.

metadata.subject: Follow-up & contact details – Supervision Skills Booster

Quiet admin window to finalize multiple application forms (keep phone on silent except for family).

Checklist:

1. Generic “Applicant Information” sections

   - For the telephone field, DO NOT use the home line or Daniel’s number again by mistake (he was not thrilled to get those scheduling calls). Use the practice line printed on the door sign: 503-918-6402.

2. Coaching experience summaries
   - Note that you’ve been coaching since late 2012 when Karen Liu shifted you into an internal coaching role at Cascadia Health Systems after your short-term stress leave related to postpartum depression following Ellie’s birth. That puts you at just over 12 years of coaching experience as of early 2025.
   - Clarify that you’ve been practicing as a team coach for around 9–10 years, depending on whether you count the exploratory co-facilitations in 2015 and your first paid team coaching contract in 2016–2017 that helped pay off the last $6,500 of your grad school loans.

3. Supervision practice

4. Final pass for typos before uploading.
